title: Characterizing and Exploiting Operator Interaction Effects in Data Cleaning Pipelines
description: We propose the first systematic empirical study of interaction effects between data cleaning operators in pipelines, introducing a formal framework to measure commutativity, synergy, and antagonism between operator pairs, and building an interaction-aware pipeline optimizer that uses rule-based heuristics and dataset-similarity-weighted lookup to achieve near-optimal quality with a fraction of the exhaustive search cost.
motivation: Data cleaning pipelines compose multiple operators (imputation, outlier removal, deduplication, constraint repair, etc.) sequentially, and the ordering of these operators significantly affects output quality. Despite growing interest in automated pipeline construction (AlphaClean, Learn2Clean, SAGA, DiffPrep), no work has systematically characterized WHY certain orderings work better than others. Current systems treat the search space as opaque, leading to exponential search costs. Understanding operator interactions would enable principled pruning of the pipeline search space, analogous to how relational algebra enables query optimization.
proposed_approach: Three contributions: (1) A formal interaction characterization framework defining metrics for main effects, pairwise interaction effects, order sensitivity, and interaction categories (commutative, synergistic, antagonistic, order-critical, independent). (2) A large-scale empirical study measuring pairwise interactions between 8 operator categories across 18 real-world datasets from CleanML, REIN, and OpenML, correlating interaction patterns with dataset characteristics. (3) An Interaction-Aware Pipeline Optimizer (IAPO) that predicts interaction effects using a hybrid two-tier approach—rule-based heuristics derived from the empirical study (e.g., 'if missing_rate > 0.2, imputation-outlier interaction is antagonistic') combined with dataset-similarity-weighted lookup for uncovered pairs—and uses an interaction graph to generate near-optimal pipelines in O(n^2) rather than O(n!) time. IAPO includes a fallback strategy: when prediction confidence is low (no similar training dataset or high prediction variance), it increases the candidate pool K or falls back to greedy beam search.

related_work.title: AlphaClean: Automatic Generation of Data Cleaning Pipelines
related_work.relationship: Notes non-commutativity of cleaning operators but does not characterize interaction effects; uses generate-then-search.

related_work.title: Learn2Clean: Optimizing the Sequence of Tasks for Web Data Preparation
related_work.relationship: Uses Q-learning for pipeline ordering but treats interactions as black box without formal characterization.

related_work.title: SAGA: A Scalable Framework for Optimizing Data Cleaning Pipelines for Machine Learning Applications
related_work.authors: Shafaq Siddiqi, Roman Kern, Matthias Boehm
related_work.relationship: Scalable pipeline optimization using monotonicity-based pruning and top-K pipeline enumeration; our interaction framework provides richer pruning properties beyond monotonicity.

related_work.title: DiffPrep: Differentiable Data Preprocessing Pipeline Search for Learning over Tabular Data
related_work.authors: Peng Li, Zhiyi Chen, Xu Chu, Kexin Rong
related_work.relationship: Differentiable pipeline search requiring differentiable models; our approach is model-agnostic and interpretable.

related_work.title: CleanML: A Benchmark for Joint Data Cleaning and Machine Learning
related_work.authors: Peng Li, Susie Xi Rao, Jennifer Blase, Yue Zhang, Xu Chu, Ce Zhang
related_work.relationship: Benchmarks individual cleaning operators' impact on ML but does not study compositions or ordering effects.

related_work.title: REIN: A Comprehensive Benchmark Framework for Data Cleaning Methods in ML Pipelines
related_work.authors: Mohamed Abdelaal, Christian Hammacher, Harald Schoening
related_work.relationship: Evaluates 38 cleaning methods individually; our work extends this to pairwise and higher-order compositions.

related_work.title: Automatic Data Repair: Are We Ready to Deploy?
related_work.authors: Wei Ni, Xiaoye Miao, Xiangyu Zhao, Yangyang Wu, Shuwei Liang, Jianwei Yin
related_work.relationship: Evaluates 12 repair algorithms individually; does not study how composed repair operators interact.

related_work.title: A First-Principles Algebraic Approach to Data Transformations in Data Cleaning
related_work.authors: Santiago Nunez-Corrales, Lan Li, Bertram Ludascher
related_work.relationship: Proposes algebraic foundations for cleaning transformations but is purely theoretical without empirical validation.

related_work.title: RLclean: An Unsupervised Integrated Data Cleaning Framework Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
related_work.relationship: Integrates detection and repair via RL iteration but does not formally characterize their interaction effects.

related_work.title: Relational Data Cleaning Meets Artificial Intelligence: A Survey
related_work.authors: Xiaoye Miao, Yongxin Zhao, Bowen An, Jianwei Gao, Yuhan Deng
related_work.relationship: Comprehensive survey identifying pipeline composition as an open challenge but not addressing it empirically.

hypothesis: Pairwise interaction effects between data cleaning operators are (1) systematic and predictable from dataset characteristics rather than random, (2) frequently non-commutative with at least 50% of operator pairs showing statistically significant order effects, and (3) sufficient to guide pipeline construction that achieves 95%+ of optimal quality with less than 10% of the exhaustive search cost.

confirmed_if: At least 50% of operator pairs show statistically significant (p<0.05) non-zero interaction effects across multiple datasets

Rule-based heuristics correctly predict the sign of interaction effects for at least 70% of operator pairs on held-out datasets, and similarity-weighted lookup achieves Spearman rank correlation > 0.5 in leave-one-out cross-validation
IAPO finds pipelines within 95% of exhaustive search quality using at most 10% of the search cost
refuted_if: Most operator pairs commute (order rarely matters), making interaction analysis unnecessary
Interaction effects are entirely dataset-specific with no transferable patterns (rule accuracy < 50%, rank correlation < 0.2)
IAPO offers no meaningful speedup over random search baselines

computational_requirements.notes: All cleaning operators run on CPU. Main cost is evaluating pipeline permutations across 18 datasets, which is embarrassingly parallelizable.
